title: NOC 94111 – Plastics processing machine operators | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Canada’s plastics industry is at the forefront of the global transition to "Advanced Materials" and "Circular Economy" models. Plastics Processing Machine Operators (NOC 94111) are the technical experts who manage the high-precision machinery—such as injection moulders, extruders, and blow moulders—that produces everything from medical-grade components to sustainable automotive parts. As Canadian manufacturers shift toward biodegradable polymers and 100% recycled feedstock, international operators with experience in automated processing and digital quality control are seeing a significant surge in high-wage job offers in Ontario and British Columbia.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Category4 (Occupations usually requiring secondary school and on-the-job training)Major Group94 – Processing and manufacturing machine operatorsSub-Major Group941 – Machine operators in mineral and metal processing

duties: Machine Setup: Setting up and operating machines that extrude, mould, and form plastic materials into products or components.
Operational Control: Adjusting controls to regulate temperature, pressure, and speed of plastic processing machinery.
Quality Assurance: Inspecting finished products for defects and using precision gauges (calipers, micrometers) to verify engineering specifications.
Maintenance: Troubleshooting minor machine malfunctions and performing routine cleaning and lubrication.
2026 Update: Utilizing digital interfaces to monitor material viscosity and logging recycled feedstock ratios for sustainability certification.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is the standard requirement.
Experience: Previous experience as a labourer in the same company is often preferred.
Training: Several weeks of on-the-job training are typically provided by the employer.
Language: CLB 4 or 5 is generally sufficient for most entry-level provincial pathways.
Certifications: Mandatory 2026 WHMIS (Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System).
job_outlook: The Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS) expects a "Balanced" market nationally, but "Shortages" in high-volume manufacturing hubs. As Canada’s packaging and medical device exports increase, over 7,000 new openings are projected due to industry growth and a wave of retirements in Ontario and Quebec.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook for plastics machine operators is rated as Good to Very Good in Canada’s specialized manufacturing corridors:
Ontario (Toronto/Brampton/Mississauga): High demand. This region is a major North American hub for plastics manufacturing, currently facing a critical labor shortage in technical operator roles.
British Columbia: Good prospects. Driven by the growth of specialized packaging and sustainable product manufacturing in the Lower Mainland.
Quebec: Stable to Good prospects. Focused in the Montreal and Montérégie regions, supporting the aerospace and medical device sectors.
Manitoba (Winnipeg): Strong demand. Essential for the production of specialized plastic components for the agricultural and transport machinery sectors.
Why Work in Canada as a Plastics Machine Operator?
Tech-Forward Environment: Work with 2026-grade technologies, including high-speed robotic de-moulding, AI-driven thermal sensors, and sustainable polymer compounding.
Diverse Sector Opportunities: Opportunities span across healthcare (medical packaging), automotive (EV components), and eco-friendly consumer goods.
Path to PR: Frequently featured in PNP In-Demand lists and eligible for the 2026 TR to PR transition streams.
Defined Career Growth: Rapidly advance into NOC 92013 (Supervisors) or specialized Quality Assurance Inspector roles with 2–3 years of experience.
pathways: Ontario Employer Job Offer (In-Demand Skills): NOC 94111 is an eligible occupation for those with a valid job offer in Ontario’s industrial sectors.
BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled (ELSS): Provides a direct route for plastics machine operators working in regional BC communities.
TR to PR Pathway (2026): Plastics operators are classified as "Essential Manufacturing Workers," making them high-priority candidates for this 33,000-person transition quota.
Saskatchewan Hard-to-Fill Skills Pilot: A streamlined pathway for technical machine operators to obtain Permanent Residency with a provincial job offer.
